gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is version 1.0.0-seed, marked 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship'. Articles I through VI are stubbed with '[To be defined by human principal]'. The MSP's purpose is to translate the human-authored BoR into precise, machine-actionable objectives and constraints, but there is no constitutional identity to translate.

The MSP Rules section states 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules via the standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow.' The seed has no rules, meaning Nomos' second compliance gate is effectively open — all Action Proposals that pass the Arbiter scope gate are authorized without further constraint.

The world state's spine_state shows that Nomos (#200), Arbiter (#190), Senate (#220), and many other organs alternate between DEGRADED and ALIVE every ~30 seconds (missed_pong → pong_recovered). This pattern repeated continuously in the minutes leading up to the snapshot, indicating systemic health flapping rather than a transient event.

The MSP Amendment Protocol designates specific roles: Senate-Archivist writes the MSP, Senate-Legislator drafts amendments from PEMs, and Senate-Rollout executes the staging→canary→activation→rollback pipeline. The world state shows only a single organ 'Senate' with no evident sub-components or named service distinctions for these functions.

Article V of the BoR (Amendment Protocol) is a stub. The BoR is the highest constitutional document, and any changes to its scope, identity, or prohibited activities require a defined amendment process. Without this, once the human principal authors the BoR, there is no formal mechanism to evolve it, potentially freezing the DIO's foundational identity.

legacy.raw_response:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights is a scaffold with no defined articles",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) is version 1.0.0-seed, marked 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship'. Articles I through VI are stubbed with '[To be defined by human principal]'. The MSP's purpose is to translate the human-authored BoR into precise, machine-actionable objectives and constraints, but there is no constitutional identity to translate.",
      "mission_reference": "Bill of Rights — constitutional identity header: 'The following is the organism's constitutional identity... Do NOT use it to make authorization decisions'; MSP Purpose: 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules'",
      "impact": "The DIO has no defined scope of enterprise, core identity, prohibited activities, human authority reservation, or interpretation rules. Nomos (#200) and Arbiter (#190) operate against a blank constitution, making all governance enforcement vacuous. The organism is adrift without a constitutional anchor, and any future MSP rules would lack a foundational document to be bound by.",
      "criticality": 10,
      "urgency": 10,
      "impact_score": 10,
      "priority": 1000
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "No substantive MSP rules exist — default-permissive mode is active",
      "description": "The MSP Rules section states 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules via the standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow.' The seed has no rules, meaning Nomos' second compliance gate is effectively open — all Action Proposals that pass the Arbiter scope gate are authorized without further constraint.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Rules: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation...'",
      "impact": "Without MSP rules, there are no operational guardrails, invariants, or safety rules. Any AP within the empty BoR perimeter (which itself is undefined) is permitted. This leaves the DIO vulnerable to unintended, harmful, or unrecoverable actions. The system is running with no operational constitution, and the gap will persist until the Senate can publish rules through the amendment pipeline.",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 9,
      "impact_score": 9,
      "priority": 729
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Governance organs (Nomos, Arbiter, Senate) are unstable, with rapid DEGRADED/ALIVE oscillations",
      "description": "The world state's spine_state shows that Nomos (#200), Arbiter (#190), Senate (#220), and many other organs alternate between DEGRADED and ALIVE every ~30 seconds (missed_pong → pong_recovered). This pattern repeated continuously in the minutes leading up to the snapshot, indicating systemic health flapping rather than a transient event.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Purpose: 'Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication... two compliance gates: (1) Arbiter scope gate (BoR perimeter), (2) MSP + genome compliance gate.' Also Amendment Protocol depends on nominal operation of these organs.",
      "impact": "The organs critical for adjudicating Action Proposals and processing Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) are unreliable. APs may be delayed, lost, or improperly evaluated during DEGRADED windows. The PEM → Legislator → Rollout pipeline for creating MSP rules cannot operate reliably, blocking the path to exiting default-permissive mode. This stalls the entire governance bootstrap process.",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 8,
      "impact_score": 9,
      "priority": 648
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "Senate organ (#220) lacks distinguishable sub-roles (Archivist, Legislator, Rollout) required by MSP",
      "description": "The MSP Amendment Protocol designates specific roles: Senate-Archivist writes the MSP, Senate-Legislator drafts amendments from PEMs, and Senate-Rollout executes the staging→canary→activation→rollback pipeline. The world state shows only a single organ 'Senate' with no evident sub-components or named service distinctions for these functions.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Amendment Protocol: 'Amendments originate only from PEMs... Senate-Legislator drafts the amendment as a minimal diff... Senate-Rollout then executes the deterministic staging → canary → activation → rollback pipeline.'",
      "impact": "Even if the organs were stable, the internal roles needed to process amendments are not clearly instantiated. The PEM→review→Rollout flow relies on these distinct actors; without them, amendments cannot be drafted, reviewed, or deployed, effectively locking the MSP in its current empty state indefinitely. This is a structural gap in the governance implementation.",
      "criticality": 7,
      "urgency": 7,
      "impact_score": 8,
      "priority": 392
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "Bill of Rights amendment protocol is undefined",
      "description": "Article V of the BoR (Amendment Protocol) is a stub. The BoR is the highest constitutional document, and any changes to its scope, identity, or prohibited activities require a defined amendment process. Without this, once the human principal authors the BoR, there is no formal mechanism to evolve it, potentially freezing the DIO's foundational identity.",
      "mission_reference": "Bill of Rights, Article V — Amendment Protocol: '[To be defined by human principal]'",
      "impact": "If the BoR is authored but later needs refinement (e.g., to add new prohibited activities or adjust enterprise scope), there is no prescribed process. This risks either uncontrolled changes (violating the human-only authority reservation) or a complete inability to adapt the constitutional identity over time. The BoR amendment vacuum is a long-term structural risk.",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact_score": 7,
      "priority": 140
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold with zero constitutional content",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Bill of Rights",
        "section": "Articles I–VI",
        "detail": "All six articles — Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, and Interpretation Rules — are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The BoR is the constitutional foundation from which the MSP derives all machine-actionable objectives, invariants, and constraints. It is the first of Nomos's two compliance gates: '(1) Arbiter scope gate (BoR perimeter), (2) MSP + genome compliance gate.' With an empty BoR, the scope gate has no perimeter to enforce, and Arbiter (#190) has nothing substantive to verify beyond a hash of an empty document."
      },
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "ARCHITECTURAL",
      "why_strategic": "Without a BoR, the entire governance architecture is nominal only. The MSP explicitly states it 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules' — but there is nothing to translate. Every Action Proposal adjudicated by Nomos (#200) passes through a scope gate that is constitutionally empty. The DIO has no defined scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, and no reserved human authorities. This is the root strategic gap from which all other governance gaps flow.",
      "radiant_evidence": "The memory blocks contain extensive architectural vision (DIO organ architecture, 28 organs, service scope extraction, bus-driven reactive architecture) and operational decisions (12-hour freshness threshold, backup patterns, autoheal pipeline) — all made and executed without a constitutional BoR perimeter. The system is building itself at high velocity inside a governance vacuum."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "title": "MSP Rules section is empty — DIO operates in unbounded default-permissive mode",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Rules",
        "detail": "The Rules section contains only '(none)' with the annotation: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules via the standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow.' No operational invariants, constraints, or safety rules exist. Nomos's second compliance gate — the 'MSP + genome compliance gate' — evaluates against an empty ruleset."
      },
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "SECURITY",
      "why_strategic": "Default-permissive mode means every Action Proposal that passes the (empty) BoR scope gate is automatically authorized by the MSP gate. There are zero machine-enforced safety invariants, no risk-tier constraints, no operational boundaries codified in machine-actionable form. The MSP is intended as the 'operational constitution' — it is currently a blank page. Senate (#220) has not yet published any substantive rules, and the amendment pipeline (PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout) has never been exercised for rule creation. This is especially acute given the velocity of architectural change: 18 services extracted, 28 organs designed, Spine/CAEB elevated, Autoheal three-layer pipeline deployed, syntra scaffolded — all under a permissive-empty MSP.",
      "radiant_evidence": "Memory blocks document extensive platform construction: CAEB/Spine elevation with WebSocket push layer, event schema registry, producer/consumer migration, Autoheal classifier and fixer with safety guardrails, Syntra project scaffold, capture processor bug fixes. All of these architectural decisions and code changes were made without MSP rule constraints. The '6 safety guardrails' in Autoheal are application-level, not MSP-level invariants — they are not evaluated by Nomos at the constitutional gate."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "title": "Dream cycle scheduler is disabled and Radiant health monitoring has never run",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Purpose",
        "detail": "The MSP states it translates the BoR into 'machine-actionable objectives' that Nomos evaluates. Radiant's dream cycle — the mechanism for context freshness triage (Phase 1) and memory consolidation (Phase 2) — is the operational realization of memory/context formation objectives. The Radiant stats block shows: 'scheduled: disabled', 'last_dream: null', 'health: never_run', and 'watchdog: { alert_count: 0, last_alert_at: null, last_check_at: null, grace_ms: 129600000 }'. The watchdog has never been activated and the health subsystem has never executed."
      },
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "OPERATIONAL",
      "why_strategic": "Memory and context formation are foundational to the DIO's identity as a 'persistent state machine.' Without autonomous dream cycles, context blocks accumulate without triage (stale, duplicate, and superseded entries persist), Phase 2 consolidation never runs outside interactive Claude Code sessions, and memory decay is unmanaged. Evidence in recent context: Dream Cycle #12 and #13 both show 'Phase 2 (Memory Consolidation): PENDING — Status: deferred to next interactive Claude Code session.' This means the DIO's memory consolidation is gated on human-initiated interactive sessions, violating the principle of autonomous operation. The 36-hour watchdog grace period has never been armed, so there is no alerting if dream cycles stall indefinitely. The MSP's purpose is to define machine-actionable objectives — autonomous memory hygiene should be among the most fundamental of these, yet it has no scheduled execution and no health monitoring.",
      "radiant_evidence": "Stats: scheduled=disabled, last_dream=null, health=never_run. Context blocks: Dream Cycle #15 (Phase 2 ran via scheduled task but used 'direct psql with equivalent logic' because 'MCP tools not available in scheduled task session' — semantic similarity scan skipped). Dream Cycles #12, #13 both show Phase 2 deferred to interactive Claude Code. Five context blocks expiring within 48h without autonomous renewal. The gap is structural: Phase 2 requires LLM access that is only reliably available in interactive sessions, creating a dependency on human presence for memory consolidation."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-004",
      "title": "No genome documents registered — organs operate without risk-tier-specific constraints",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Genome References",
        "detail": "The Genome References section states: 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' The current entry is: '(none at seed)'. No organ-specific genome documents have been created or registered, despite 28 organs being architected and multiple probabilistic services (MCB, Radiant, Minder, Autoheal, Syntra) actively operating."
      },
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "MEDIUM",
      "impact": "ARCHITECTURAL",
      "why_strategic": "Genome documents are the mechanism for translating the MSP's general rules into organ-specific operating constraints with risk-tier differentiation. The vision document (memory block bab3c3b5) defines 28 active organs across 10 categories, including probabilistic services that 'are strictly controlled black boxes with internal service agents and service orchestrators.' Without genome documents, these probabilistic services — which by definition use LLMs and carry elevated risk — have no codified risk-tier constraints evaluated by Nomos. The service classification (deterministic vs probabilistic) exists as architectural documentation but is not operationalized as machine-actionable genome rules. The Autoheal fixer's '6 safety guardrails' are implemented in application code (scr-bash-llm-ops-autoheal-guards.sh), not in genome documents that Nomos evaluates at the constitutional gate. This means safety constraints are application-level conventions, not constitutional invariants.",
      "radiant_evidence": "Memory blocks document: 5 probabilistic services identified (MCB, Radiant dreamer Phase 2, Minder, Autoheal classifier+fixer, Syntra), each with LLM access. Autoheal fixer has '6 deterministic safety guardrails' implemented in bash — but these are app-level, not genome-level. The vision document explicitly states probabilistic services are 'strictly controlled black boxes' — but the 'strictly controlled' part has no constitutional encoding."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-005",
      "title": "Memory embedding coverage degraded to 72% with no autonomous remediation path",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Purpose",
        "detail": "The MSP defines the DIO's operational objectives, which implicitly include memory integrity and retrieval quality. The Radiant stats show: 'embeddings: { with: 69, without: 27, total: 96, coverage_pct: 72 }'. Twenty-seven memory entries lack vector embeddings, reducing semantic retrieval quality for memory-augmented operations. There is no MSP rule or genome constraint that defines a minimum embedding coverage threshold or triggers remediation when coverage degrades."
      },
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "MEDIUM",
      "impact": "OPERATIONAL",
      "why_strategic": "Memory retrieval quality directly impacts the DIO's ability to make context-aware decisions. Radiant, Minder, and MCB all depend on semantic memory search for their core functions. A 28% embedding gap means over a quarter of memory entries are invisible to vector similarity search, forcing fallback to metadata-only retrieval. This degrades the quality of memory consolidation (Dream Phase 2), person-relevance computation (Minder), and event interpretation (MCB). The gap has no defined remediation trigger — no PEM is generated, no Autoheal ticket is created, and no minimum threshold is codified in MSP or genome rules. The embedding gap is a leading indicator of memory system degradation that currently has no governance attention.",
      "radiant_evidence": "Stats: embeddings coverage_pct=72, 27 entries without embeddings out of 96 total. Memory blocks include consolidated entries (e.g., 'Consolidated from 8 entries on 2026-04-07') that may or may not have embeddings generated post-consolidation. The Dream Cycle #15 Phase 2 note explicitly states 'semantic similarity scan skipped (no embedding sidecar access)' — confirming that embedding-dependent operations are fragile and environment-dependent."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-006",
      "title": "Recent context formation is dominated by infrastructure failure patterns rather than strategic progress signals",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Purpose",
        "detail": "The MSP establishes the DIO's operational objectives including context freshness and memory formation. Analysis of the Radiant Slice's recent_context blocks (16 entries) shows a pattern: 9 of 16 context blocks (56%) are backup failure reports, git sync failures, or dream cycle deferrals. Only 2 context blocks capture strategic progress. The context formation pipeline is ingesting operational noise at a rate that may crowd out strategically significant signals during memory consolidation."
      },
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "LOW",
      "impact": "OPERATIONAL",
      "why_strategic": "Context blocks are the raw material for memory consolidation. When the context stream is dominated by recurring infrastructure failures (NAS mount denied, GitLab non-fast-forward, sqlite3 backup hangs), the Dream Phase 2 consolidation process — which only runs in interactive sessions — expends its limited LLM attention budget on deduplicating and consolidating failure patterns rather than advancing strategic understanding. Evidence: Memory block 8e6c090a consolidates 4 backup failure entries into a 'SafeVault Backup History' pattern. Memory block 307ec510 consolidates 2 git sync failures. These are valuable consolidations, but the formation of strategic memory (architectural decisions, platform identity, vision evolution) is occurring in interactive sessions and being promoted directly to memory without the same consolidation pipeline. The context-to-memory pipeline has an implicit bias toward operational incident recording at the expense of strategic signal amplification.",
      "radiant_evidence": "Recent context: 9/16 blocks are failure/error reports (backup failures on 2026-05-03, 2026-05-01, 2026-04-30, 2026-04-28, 2026-04-27, 2026-04-04; git sync failures on 2026-04-09, 2026-04-03 ×2). Dream cycle deferrals appear in cycles #12, #13. Strategic content (MP-5 organ rename cascade, Dream Cycle #15 completion) represents only 2/16 context blocks. The memory tier, by contrast, is rich with architectural decisions and strategic vision — suggesting these are being promoted via a different path (interactive session direct promotion) rather than through the autonomous context→consolidation→memory pipeline."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-007",
      "title": "MSP amendment pipeline has never been exercised — no operational precedent for constitutional evolution",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Amendment Protocol",
        "detail": "The Amendment Protocol defines the only path for MSP evolution: 'Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos (#200) when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints.' The pipeline is: PEM → Senate-Legislator draft → review state → HOM notification → POST /amend/review with decision=approve → Senate-Rollout staging → canary → activation → rollback. The version history shows only one entry: '1.0.0-seed — 2026-04-10 — (bootstrap) — Empty seed published by Senate-Archivist on first boot.' No PEM has triggered an amendment because no AP has been blocked — the MSP rules are empty, so nothing blocks."
      },
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "MEDIUM",
      "impact": "GOVERNANCE",
      "why_strategic": "The amendment pipeline is a critical constitutional mechanism that has never been tested. When the BoR is authored and MSP rules are established, the pipeline must function correctly: Nomos must generate valid PEMs, Senate-Legislator must draft minimal diffs, the HOM notification must reach the human principal, the approval endpoint must work, and Senate-Rollout must execute the deterministic staging→canary→activation→rollback pipeline. None of these components have been exercised end-to-end. Additionally, the amendment protocol specifies that 'Senate-Legislator may amend this document through the PEM → review → Rollout pipeline, but may never use MSP amendments to expand enterprise scope. Scope expansion requires a BoR amendment, which is human-only.' This constraint chain — PEM to MSP amendment to BoR amendment escalation — has zero operational precedent. When the first real PEM fires, the entire governance machinery will be tested for the first time in a potentially high-stakes situation.",
      "radiant_evidence": "No PEMs exist in recent context or memory. The MSP version history confirms only the bootstrap seed exists. The Senate (#220), Senate-Legislator, Senate-Archivist, and Senate-Rollout organs are architected (per vision document memory bab3c3b5) but their amendment pipeline interactions have never been exercised because the empty MSP rules never block any AP."
    }
  ]
}
```
